arguments.task: Open the official DealCloud API documentation and first-party DealCloud/Intapp pages needed to verify: one-line product description, documented authentication methods, API surface type, any rate limit notes, and whether access is self-serve or gated. Also check official pricing, sales/contact, or developer pages for explicit gating language. Separately check whether an official MCP server/integration is documented by DealCloud or Intapp. Return the exact URLs visited and concise extracted evidence for each field. Do not rely on search result snippets; open and read the pages.

input.thought: Start a browser-based review of official documentation and first-party pages for DealCloud research.

Then open any first-party access/contact or licensing page reachable from docs or main site that loads successfully. Return a final summary with exact URLs visited and quoted evidence for: product description, auth methods, REST/GraphQL surface, rate limits, MCP existence, and whether API/MCP access requires existing DealCloud permissions or license.

raw_result.description: DealCloud is an Intapp platform with a documented REST API for accessing and integrating DealCloud data and schema.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official DealCloud API docs include an API Keys page and an Authorization Token page describing a bearer token obtained via OAuth 2.0 client credentials.
raw_result.gating_reason: The API keys documentation indicates keys are generated in-app by users with API permissions, so access depends on an existing DealCloud tenant and admin-enabled permissions rather than open self-serve signup.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs expose a REST API with guides and references for authentication, limits, data, and schema endpoints; the available evidence supports a meaningful but not fully inventoried integration surface.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official docs include a DealCloud MCP Server preview/getting-started section; available evidence indicates it requires a 'DealCloud with Celeste' license.

raw_result.blocker: A first toolkit appears buildable from the public REST docs, but real use requires an existing DealCloud environment plus API permissions and MCP may require a specific license.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official limits page indicates 300 requests per minute for authentication endpoints and 5 requests per second for core endpoints such as data/schema.
raw_result.notable_signals: Docs prominently include API references, guides, limits, and an official MCP server preview, which is a strong signal of active platform investment.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The accessible first-party evidence supports auth, REST API, rate limits, and MCP existence, but the strongest public product description and commercial gating language on Intapp marketing pages could not be verified because the first-party marketing page returned 403 during browsing. Access tier and description are therefore inferred from docs-side evidence rather than directly confirmed by a readable pricing/contact page.
